subject: Ecology
title: What Is Soil?
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on What Is Soil?.

hook.text: Soil is not dirt. It's a living ecosystem that took centuries to build one centimeter.

buildup.text: Soil forms when rock weathers, organic matter accumulates, and organisms move in.

discovery.text: A single teaspoon of healthy soil contains more microorganisms than people on Earth.

twist.text: Without soil, there would be no land plants, no food crops, and no terrestrial ecosystems.

climax.text: We depend on a thin skin of soil—rarely more than a meter deep—for nearly everything we eat.

punchline.text: Civilization stands on a meter of soil.

quiz.question: How long can it take to form one centimeter of soil?
